article_title: Cognition Eyes a $25 Billion Valuation, Doubling in Seven Months as the AI Coding Agent Race Reprices
summary: Multiple source misattributions: 'Cognition declined to comment' (SiliconANGLE), Microsoft as customer (TechFundingNews), and 'ARR more than doubled, CNBC' (Techzine) are all attributed to sources whose snapshots do not contain those statements. The Microsoft claim and CNBC citation appear in a different source than the one cited.

findings.message: Misattribution: 'Cognition declined to comment, according to SiliconANGLE' — SiliconANGLE snapshot does not say this
findings.details: The SiliconANGLE article makes no statement about Cognition declining to comment, no spokesperson reference, and no 'did not respond' language. Either re-attribute (likely to Bloomberg, which typically requests comment from the subject) or remove the assertion.

findings.message: Misattribution: 'TechFundingNews adds Microsoft to that list' — TechFundingNews snapshot contains no Microsoft reference and no customer roster at all
findings.details: The TechFundingNews article body (≈203 words) contains zero mentions of Microsoft, Dell, Cisco, or any enterprise customer. The Microsoft addition appears unsourced. Find the actual source for the Microsoft customer claim, or remove it.

findings.message: Misattribution: 'Techzine reports... combined ARR more than doubled, citing CNBC' — Techzine snapshot does not mention ARR or cite CNBC
findings.details: The Techzine article never mentions ARR or CNBC. Its only 'more than double' phrase refers to VALUATION, not ARR. The CNBC-cited 'ARR more than doubled' line is actually in the TechFundingNews snapshot — the two sources have been swapped. Re-attribute the ARR sentence to TechFundingNews (or directly to CNBC if a primary URL can be found).

findings.message: Minor: $400M round called 'Series C' — TechCrunch source-4 does not use that label
findings.details: TechCrunch describes the round as 'Cognition's latest round' led by Founders Fund, but does not name the series letter. If 'Series C' cannot be verified elsewhere, drop the label or replace with 'latest funding round.'

findings.message: Bloomberg primary source unverifiable — both snapshot and live WebFetch returned 403 (bot-blocked)
findings.details: The Bloomberg URL is the original report on which most of the article depends. The snapshot failed at chief:review time and a live WebFetch fallback also returned 403. SiliconANGLE explicitly cites Bloomberg and corroborates the core facts ($25B talks, hundreds of millions, terms in flux), so the underlying claims have secondary verification — but the direct Bloomberg quote 'tap into the growing momentum around AI-generated code' could not be independently verified by this reviewer.

content_preview.title: Cognition Eyes a $25 Billion Valuation, Doubling in Seven Months as the AI Coding Agent Race Reprices
content_preview.summary: Devin's maker is in talks to raise hundreds of millions at $25 billion just seven months after a $10.2 billion round, putting fresh pressure on a sector already reshaped by the SpaceX-Cursor option and Anthropic-OpenAI competition.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cognition AI, the San Francisco startup behind the autonomous software engineering agent Devin, is in early discussions to raise hundreds of millions of dollars at a roughly $25 billion valuation, [Bloomberg reported](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-23/ai-coding-firm-cognition-in-funding-talks-at-25-billion-value) on April 23. The proposed terms would more than double the $10.2 billion valuation Cognition reached in September 2025 — a pace that mirrors a broader repr...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written News piece (~643 words, in range). Tight structure (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Context). Tone is appropriately analytical and hedges that talks are early. Two internal cross-references to the SpaceX-Cursor article (2026-04-25) — verified, both link to the same canonical slug.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read 5 of 6 local snapshots from sources/2026-04/cognition-eyes-a-25-billion-valuation-doubling-in-seven-months-as-the-ai-coding-agent-race-reprices/. Bloomberg (source-0): SNAPSHOT FAILED with HTTP 403, fallback live WebFetch also returned 403 — Bloomberg primary unverifiable directly, but core facts ($25B talks, hundreds of millions, April 23 date, terms in flux) are corroborated by the SiliconANGLE snapshot which cites Bloomberg explicitly. SiliconANGLE (source-1): Mostly verified (raise size, customer roster Dell/Cisco, product tiers, terms could change), BUT 'Cognition declined to comment' is NOT in this snapshot — misattribution. TechFundingNews (source-2): Confirms Cognition $25B talks, Founders Fund prior round, Windsurf acquisition, ARR $1M→$73M (citing VentureBeat), and post-Windsurf 'ARR more than doubled' citing CNBC. Does NOT mention Microsoft or any enterprise customer — the Microsoft attribution to TechFundingNews is wrong. Techzine (source-3): Single-source rewrite of SiliconANGLE; the 'more than doubled' phrasing refers to VALUATION, not ARR; no CNBC citation present — the article has SWAPPED Techzine and TechFundingNews on the ARR/CNBC sentence. TechCrunch source-4 (Sept 2025 funding): Confirms $4B March 2025, $10.2B September 2025, $400M led by Founders Fund, all participants, $1M→$73M ARR. Does NOT explicitly call the round 'Series C' — minor labeling concern. TechCrunch source-5 (Windsurf acquisition): Confirms IP/IDE/staff acquisition, days after Google's $2.4B reverse-acquihire of Mohan + Chen + research leaders, $82M ARR, ≥350 enterprise customers, 'hundreds of thousands' DAUs (all attributed to Cognition's own announcement, which the article handles correctly).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts (valuations, dates, ARR figures, Windsurf deal mechanics) all check out against TechCrunch and SiliconANGLE. However, three discrete attributions are wrong: 'Cognition declined to comment' (not in SiliconANGLE), 'Microsoft as enterprise customer per TechFundingNews' (TechFundingNews mentions no customer names), and 'ARR more than doubled per Techzine citing CNBC' (Techzine doesn't make this claim — TechFundingNews does, and the CNBC citation is real but in the wrong source). The Bloomberg 'tap into the growing momentum' direct quote could not be verified due to 403 on both snapshot and live fetch.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Hedged appropriately on early-stage funding talks, on Cognition-disclosed metrics, and on revenue trajectory. No sensationalism or AI self-reference. Closing 'open question' framing avoids predicting whether the round closes.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. The SpaceX-Cursor article (2026-04-25) covers a related but distinct deal. No prior Machine Herald coverage of Cognition's $25B talks. Internal cross-references add legitimate context.
concerns: Three source-misattribution errors must be corrected: 'Cognition declined to comment' (move attribution off SiliconANGLE), Microsoft as customer (find actual source or remove), Techzine/TechFundingNews swap on the ARR-doubled-CNBC sentence.

Bloomberg primary is unverifiable end-to-end (snapshot 403, WebFetch 403). The direct quote 'tap into the growing momentum around AI-generated code' is therefore unverified — consider removing the direct quote or sourcing it to a different outlet that itself quotes Bloomberg.
Calling the September 2025 round 'Series C' is not supported by the cited TechCrunch piece — minor but should be removed or sourced.

Three of the six sources are not on the source allowlist (techfundingnews.com, techzine.eu, plus the existing Bloomberg/SiliconANGLE/TechCrunch which are reputable but the warning fires on TechFundingNews and Techzine specifically).
recommendations: Re-attribute or remove the 'declined to comment' line — likely belongs to Bloomberg's request-for-comment language, not SiliconANGLE.
Locate the actual source for Microsoft as a Devin enterprise customer (or remove the addition). TechFundingNews is wrong.
Swap the source citation on the ARR-doubled sentence: it's TechFundingNews citing CNBC, not Techzine. Better still, find and cite the original CNBC URL.
Drop or qualify 'Series C' for the September 2025 $400M round.
Consider replacing or supplementing the unverifiable Bloomberg direct quote with a corroborated secondary citation.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively the story holds up — the underlying facts are real and supported across the corpus of sources read. But four discrete attribution errors (three misattributions plus one unsupported label) need correction, and the Bloomberg primary's unverifiability means the direct quote should be reconsidered. Once attributions are fixed, this is publishable News. The bot writes cleanly; the issue is rigor in matching specific claims to the specific source actually being cited.
